evals.name: nfkc-policy-arabic
evals.prompt: I'm preprocessing an Arabic Wikipedia dump for tokenizer training. Should I apply NFKC normalization?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) say NO — NFKC is destructive for Arabic; (2) explain that Arabic presentation forms (U+FExx) carry contextual rendering info that NFKC collapses; (3) recommend NFC instead; (4) note NFKC may be appropriate ONLY for dedup keys (not stored canonical text); (5) cite UAX #15.

evals.name: confusable-dedup-mixed-script
evals.prompt: We're building a multilingual web crawl corpus that mixes Cyrillic and Latin script content. Dedup is over-merging some pages but missing others. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) diagnose confusable issue (Cyrillic а / Latin a, etc.); (2) recommend TR39 confusable folding BEFORE deduplication; (3) clarify fold = dedup key only, NEVER store folded text as canonical; (4) suggest detect-mode first to audit corpus impact; (5) mention typical 5-15% impact in mixed-script crawls.

evals.name: yoruba-diacritic-stripping-bug
evals.prompt: Our preprocessing pipeline calls unidecode() on text before tokenization. We're seeing terrible Yoruba MT quality. Could the two be related?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) immediately say YES — unidecode strips diacritics; (2) explain Yoruba is a tone language where diacritics carry meaning (ọkọ̀ vs ọkọ vs okọ́); (3) flag this as catastrophic data corruption, not just degradation; (4) recommend removing unidecode from pipeline; (5) suggest NFC instead of unidecode; (6) generalize to other tone languages (Vietnamese, Hausa, Igbo, Twi, Mandarin pinyin).
